seo.title: Convert PDF to Word Free – No Sign Up Required | SmallPDF.us
seo.description: Convert PDF files to fully editable Word documents (.docx) online for free. Layout, images and formatting preserved. Fast, secure, no software to install.

heroSubtitle: Turn your PDF files into fully editable Word documents — no quality loss, no account needed. Upload your file and download the result in moments.
dropSubtitle: or click to browse files from your device
maxSizeNote: Max file size: 50 MB · Up to 10 files at once

featuresSubtitle: Our technology digs deep into every PDF to produce a Word document that stays as close to the original as possible.
feature1Desc: Every paragraph, heading, list, and column is pulled out with precision. The text in your Word file faithfully mirrors the content of the original PDF.
feature2Desc: Even PDFs with dozens of pages convert in seconds. No frustrating waits, no frozen browser — everything happens quietly in the background.
feature3Desc: Your files are used solely for conversion, then automatically deleted from our servers after one hour. We never read or permanently store your content.
feature4Desc: Embedded images, logos, and diagrams are carried over into the Word document at exactly the same position as in the original PDF.
feature5Desc: Whether you're on iPhone, Android, or a tablet, our tool runs in any modern browser — no app download, no fuss.
feature6Desc: No software to download, no account to create, no credit card needed. Open the site and start converting right now.
stepsSubtitle: Three simple steps to turn your PDF into an editable Word document
step1Desc: Drag and drop one or more PDF files into the upload area, or click to browse your device. Up to 10 files at 50 MB each in a single go.
step2Desc: Our system identifies text blocks, layout, images, and tables to rebuild a cleanly structured Word document that's ready to edit.
step3Desc: Your fully editable .docx file is ready in seconds. Open it in Microsoft Word, Google Docs, or LibreOffice — it's all yours.
faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about converting PDF to Word
faq1Question: How accurate is the PDF to Word conversion?
faq1Answer: It depends on the type of PDF. For digitally created PDFs — exported from Word, InDesign, or similar — the conversion is highly accurate: text, formatting, and structure all come through cleanly. For scanned PDFs without embedded text, accuracy can vary because an OCR (optical character recognition) step is required.
faq2Question: Will my document's layout be preserved?
faq2Answer: Yes. We work hard to reproduce the original layout as faithfully as possible. Columns, headings, lists, and embedded images are all accounted for. Very complex layouts — like multi-column magazine spreads — may occasionally show minor differences.
faq3Answer: Absolutely. All transfers are encrypted via HTTPS/SSL. Your files are never stored permanently or shared with any third party — they're automatically deleted from our servers after one hour.
faq4Question: How many PDF files can I convert at once?
faq4Answer: You can upload up to 10 PDF files at a time, each up to 50 MB. Batch conversion is a huge time-saver when you have a stack of documents to process in one go.
faq5Question: Can I convert the Word file back to PDF afterward?
faq5Answer: Of course. The downloaded .docx file can be opened and edited in Microsoft Word, Google Docs, or LibreOffice, then exported back to PDF or other formats like ODT or TXT. You have complete control over the document.
